rule.desc: Avoid type comments. Use modern annotation syntax instead.
rule.rationale: Type comments are legacy syntax for Python <3.6. Modern annotations are clearer.

example.code: # Good - modern annotations (Python 3.6+) def process(data: list[int]) -> int: total: int = 0 items: list[str] = [] return total
# Bad - type comments (legacy) def process(data): # type: (list[int]) -> int total = 0 # type: int items = [] # type: list[str] return total

rule.tool_support: Modern annotations work in Python 3.6+. Use them exclusively.
